title: Tool Dispatch & Execution Flow
activity_flow: START: agent_runner_loop receives LLM response with tool_calls
Parse tool_calls → list of {tool_name, args, id}
[branch] tool_name != 'no_tool' → call handler.dispatch(tool_name, args, response, index=ii)
Call try_call_generator(tool_before_callback, tool_name, args, response) → prer
Call try_call_generator(getattr(self, method_name), args, response) → ret
Call try_call_generator(tool_after_callback, tool_name, args, response, ret) → _
[NO, other unknown tool] yield '未知工具: {tool_name}'; Return StepOutcome(None, next_prompt='未知工具 {tool_name}', should_exit=False)
Drive generator: next(gen) to get first yielded value, then proxy yields remaining output
[YES] Set exit_reason = {result: 'EXITED', data: outcome.data}; BREAK loop
[YES] Set exit_reason = {result: 'CURRENT_TASK_DONE', data: outcome.data}; BREAK loop
[decision] outcome.data is not None and tool_name != 'no_tool'?
[YES, _done_hooks available] Pop hook → add to next_prompts
[YES, no hooks] BREAK outer while loop
Call turn_end_callback(response, tool_calls, tool_results, turn, joined_next_prompts, exit_reason) → next_prompt
Build new messages = [{role: user, content: next_prompt, tool_results: tool_results}]
Continue to next turn (back to LLM call)

key_methods.BaseHandler.dispatch: dispatch(self, tool_name, args, response, index=0) → generator yielding strings, returns StepOutcome
key_methods.try_call_generator: try_call_generator(func, *args, **kwargs) → generator; transparently wraps both plain-return and generator functions
key_methods.tool_before_callback: tool_before_callback(self, tool_name, args, response) → pass (no-op in BaseHandler, overridable)
key_methods.tool_after_callback: tool_after_callback(self, tool_name, args, response, ret) → pass (no-op in BaseHandler, overridable)
key_methods.turn_end_callback: turn_end_callback(self, response, tool_calls, tool_results, turn, next_prompt, exit_reason) → next_prompt string; overridden in GenericAgentHandler with history/plan-mode logic

do_methods_in_GenericAgentHandler: do_code_run(args, response) → yields progress, returns StepOutcome(result)
do_ask_user(args, response) → yields wait msg, returns StepOutcome(result, should_exit=True)
do_web_scan(args, response) → yields info, returns StepOutcome(result)
do_web_execute_js(args, response) → yields JS result, returns StepOutcome(result)
do_file_patch(args, response) → yields action/status, returns StepOutcome(result)
do_file_write(args, response) → yields action/status, returns StepOutcome(result)
do_file_read(args, response) → yields action, returns StepOutcome(result)
do_update_working_checkpoint(args, response) → yields info, returns StepOutcome(result)
do_no_tool(args, response) → handles no-tool turns, plan mode, code-block detection; returns StepOutcome(response, next_prompt=None) for final answer
do_start_long_term_update(args, response) → triggers memory distillation flow
branching_conditions: tool_name == 'bad_json' → error StepOutcome with msg, no exit
unknown tool → yield error string, StepOutcome with 未知工具 prompt
outcome.should_exit → exit immediately (e.g., do_ask_user returns should_exit=True)
not outcome.next_prompt → exit as CURRENT_TASK_DONE (e.g., do_no_tool final answer)
outcome.next_prompt starts with '未知工具' → reset LLM tools cache
outcome.data not None → serialize and append to tool_results for next LLM turn
output_streaming: do_* methods are generators; intermediate yield strings are streamed to UI; final return value is StepOutcome captured via StopIteration
important_notes: dispatch is itself a generator (uses yield from try_call_generator); caller must drive it
try_call_generator unifies plain-return and generator do_* methods transparently
tool_before/after_callbacks are hooks for cross-cutting concerns (logging, validation) — currently no-ops
verbose mode: wraps tool output in backtick fences and streams each yielded chunk; non-verbose: exhaust() drains generator silently
args['_index'] injected by dispatch to let do_* methods know their position in multi-tool turns
_done_hooks: list of deferred prompts appended after a turn exits, allowing chained tasks
